name: Holmium
name_origin: From Holmia, the Latin name for Stockholm, the home city of one of its discoverers.

discovery.discoverer: Per Teodor Cleve; independently by Marc Delafontaine and Jacques-Louis Soret

occurrence.abundance_summary: Moderately rare lanthanide (~1.3 ppm in crust); recovered as a minor component of monazite, gadolinite, and xenotime ores.

compounds.name: Holmium(III) oxide
compounds.summary: Pale-yellow to pinkish solid (colour depends on illumination); used as a calibration standard for optical spectrophotometers.

compounds.name: Holmium(III) chloride
compounds.summary: Yellow hygroscopic salt; precursor to holmium metal and to holmium-doped laser host materials.

compounds.name: Holmium(III) fluoride
compounds.summary: Used in fluoride glass fibres and as a starting material for high-purity holmium metal.

compounds.name: Holmium-doped yttrium aluminium garnet
compounds.summary: Active medium for ~2.1 micrometre lasers used in surgery and dentistry.

uses: Active dopant in 2.1 micrometre Ho:YAG and Ho:YLF lasers used in medical surgery and dentistry
Pole-piece material for the highest-field static permanent magnets
Magnetostrictive applications and high-temperature flux concentrators
Calibration standard for UV-visible spectrophotometers (Ho2O3 glass filter)
Yellow / red colourant in cubic zirconia and specialty glass
Neutron absorber in nuclear reactor control alloys
reactivity_summary: Soft, silvery-white ductile metal that is stable in dry air at room temperature but rapidly oxidises in moist air or at elevated temperatures. Reacts slowly with cold water, more vigorously with hot water and dilute acids.
isotopes_summary: Holmium has one stable, naturally occurring isotope (Ho-165). The metastable nuclear isomer Ho-166m1 (half-life ~1200 years) and Ho-163 (~4570 years) are the longest-lived synthetic isotopes; Ho-166 is used in radiotherapy.

citations.source: Royal Society of Chemistry - Periodic Table: Holmium
IUPAC Periodic Table of the Elements (2021)
CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 102nd Edition
